debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
R : Type u_2
mα : MeasurableSpace α
mR : MeasurableSpace R
inst✝⁶ : Nonempty α
inst✝⁵ : StandardBorelSpace α
inst✝⁴ : DecidableEq α
inst✝³ : Countable α
inst✝² : StandardBorelSpace R
inst✝¹ : Nonempty R
alg : Algorithm α R
ν : Kernel α R
inst✝ : IsMarkovKernel ν
n : ℕ
⊢ IndepFun (fun ω => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x => @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n)
    (@Bandits.ArrayModel.arrayMeasure α R mα mR ν)

failed_attempts:
have h₁ : MeasurableSpace (probSpace α R) := inferInstance
  have h₂ : MeasurableSpace (ℕ → I) := inferInstance
  have h₃ : MeasurableSpace (ℕ → α → R) := inferInstance
  have h₄ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₅ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) (Measure.prod (Measure.infinitePi fun _ => volume) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)) := by
      have h₆ : IndepFun (fun ω : ℕ → I => ω (n + 1)) (fun ω : ℕ → α → R => ω) (Measure.infinitePi fun _ => volume) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) := by
        -- Use the fact that the random variables at different indices are independent in the product measure
        apply Independent.induced
        · exact independent_infinitePi_different n (n + 1)
        · exact independent_infinitePi_of_constant
      -- Extend the independence to the product measure
      simpa [Measure.infinitePi, Measure.prod] using h₆.prod
    exact h₅
  -- Conclude the proof by showing the required independence
  have h₇ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₈ : Measurable (fun x : probSpace α R => (x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R)).2) := by
      have h₉ : Measurable (fun x : probSpace α R => (x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R)).2) := by
        have h₁₀ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => x.2) := by
          exact Measurable.prod_right
        exact h₁₀.comp (by simp [Measurable])
      exact h₉
    have h₉ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω ↦ ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := h₄
    have h₁₀ : Measurable (fun x : probSpace α R => @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) := by
      -- Prove that the posection of the history is measurable
      apply measurable_hist
      <;> try norm_num
      <;> try simp_all
      <;> try aesop
    -- Use the fact that the function f is measurable and the independence of the two functions
    have h₁₁ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      refine' h₉.of_sub( _
        , _
        , _
        )
      <;> (try aesop)
      <;> (try simp_all)
      <;> (try measurable)
      <;> (try norm_num)
    exact h₁₁
  exact h₇

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      -- Use the fact that the first component of the probability space is independent of the second component
      have h₂ : (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) = (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω.1) ∘ (Function.eval (n + 1)) := rfl
      have h₃ : (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω) = id := rfl
      rwa [h₂, h₃] using indepFun_fst_snd ν
      <;> simp
      <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₁
  -- Use the fact that the function (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω) is ⨂-measurable
  have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n)
    (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₃ : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω) (arrayMeasure ν) := h
    have h₄ : (fun x : probSpace α R ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) ≤ (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω) := by
      -- Prove that (fun x : probSpace α R ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) is ≤ (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω)
      refine' fun A hA ↦ ?
      have h₅ : (fun x : probSpace α R ↦ @Bandits ArrayModel hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) ⁻¹' A ⊆ (fun ω : probSpace α R ↦ ω) ⁻¹' A := by
        intro x hx
        simp only [Set.mem_preimage] at hx ⊢
        aesop
      exact h₅
    exact h₃.of_mono_right h₄
  exact h₂

refine indepFun_map_right _ _
  · -- Prove that applyMapRight is measurable
    exact measurable_comp_angle (by fun_prop)
  · -- Prove that applyMapRight is measurable
    exact measurable_comp_angle (by fun_prop)
  · -- Prove that the first component and the second component are independent
    simp_all [arrayMeasure, Measure.prod_map happening, Measure.independent_prod _ _]
    <;>
    (try infer_instance) <;>
    (try simp_all [indepFun_iff_indep, MeasurableEquiv]) <;>
    (try
      {
        -- Use the fact that the components are independent and the properties of the array measure
        simp_all [indepFun_iff_indep, MeasurableEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
        <;>
        (try
          {
            -- Use the properties of the product measure and the array measure
            apply independent_prod_left_right
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            -- Use the fact that the components are independent and the properties of the array measure
            simp_all [indepFun_iff_indep, MeasurableEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
            <;>
            (try
              {
                -- Use the properties of the product measure and the array measure
                apply independent_prod_left_right
              })
          })
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        -- Use the fact that the components are independent and the properties of the array measure
        simp_all [indepFun_iff_indep, MeasurableEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
        <;>
        (try
          {
            -- Use the properties of the product measure and the array measure
            apply independent_prod_left_right
          })
      })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Use the properties of the product measure and the array measure
      apply independent_prod_left_right
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Use the fact that the components are independent and the properties of the array measure
      simp_all [indepFun_iff_indep, MeasurableEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the properties of the product measure and the array measure
          apply independent_prod_left_right
        })
    })

apply indepFun_prod
  have h₂ : fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n
    = fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => (fun ω : (ℕ → α → R) => ω) x.2 |>.1 := by
    simp [hist]
  rw [h₂]
  have h₃ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => (fun ω : (ℕ → α → R) => ω) ω.2 |>.1)
      (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₄ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => (fun ω : (ℕ → α → R) => ω) ω.2)
        (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      have h₅ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2)
          (arrayMeasure ν) := h₁
      have h₆ : (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => (fun ω : (ℕ → α → R) => ω) ω.2)
         = (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) := by
        rfl
      rw [h₆] at *
      exact h₅
    exact h₄.comp_right (fun ω : ℕ → α → R => (ω : ℕ → α → R) |>.1) (by fun_prop)
  exact h₃

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) ((Measure.infinitePi fun _ => volume) ×ˢ (Measure.infinitePi fun _ => volume)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
      have h₃ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) ((Measure.infinitePi fun _ => volume) ×ˢ (Measure.infinitePi fun _ => volume)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
        refine' IndepFun.prod _ _ _
        · -- Show that the first coordinate is independent of the second
          refine' IndepFun.fst_of_indefiniteProd _ _ _ _
          <;> simp_all [indefiniteProd_ind_prodMeas_fun, πi_proj, Function.update_same]
          <;> aesop
        · -- Show that the first coordinate is independent of the second
          exact IndepFun.id
        · -- Show that the first coordinate is independent of the second
          simp_all [indefiniteProd_ind_prodMeas_fun, πi_proj, Function.update_same]
          <;> aesop
      exact h₃
    -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
    exact h₂
  -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
  have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => @hist α R mα mR _ _ _ alg ω n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₃ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := h₁
    have h₄ : Measurable (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => @hist α R mα mR _ _ _ alg ω n) := by
      -- Show that the function is measurable
      apply measurable_hist
    have h₅ : (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => @hist α R mα mR _ _ _ alg ω n) = (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.2) ∘ (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => (ω.1, (fun i : ℕ => fun a : α => (fun i a => 0 : ℕ → α → ℕ) i a))) := by
      -- Show that the function is equal to the composition of two functions
      ext ω
      simp [hist]
      <;> aesop
    -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
    have h₆ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) => @hist α R mα mR _ _ _ alg ω n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
      refine' h₃.comp_right _ h₄
      <;> simp_all [h₅]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₆
  -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent in the product measure
  exact h₂

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun ω → ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω => (ω.1 : ℕ → I)) arrayMeasure ν := by
    -- Use the fact that the first component (ω.1) is an independent sequence of I-valued random variables
    refine' inst_indepFun_map_fst n _ (by simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  have h₂ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun ω => (ω.1 : ℕ → I)) ω) := by
    -- The function (ω : probSpace α R) ↦ ω.1 is measurable
    fun_prop
  -- Since (fun ω => ω.1 (n + 1)) and (fun ω => (ω.1 : ℕ → I)) are independent,
  -- and (fun ω => (ω.1 : ℕ → I)) is measurable, we can use the transitivity of independence
  refine' h₁.of_indepFun_right (IndepFun.comp_right (F := fun ω : ℕ → I => ω.natCast) _ h₂)
  -- Simplify the composition to show that it from (ω.1 : ℕ → I) to hist alg ω n
  simp [Function.comp]
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try measurable_pi_iff)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try measurable_pi_iff)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try measurable_pi_iff)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try measurable_pi_iff)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try measurable_pi_iff)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try measurable_pi_iff)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure])
  <;>
  (try fun_prop)

have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) ▸[ (Measure.independent (a := (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) ⁻¹' ?_) ) ] (fun x ↦ hist alg x n) := by
    refine' ⟨_, _⟩
    · <;> simp_all [arrayMeasure, Measure.independent, Measure.prod_null, Measure.infinitePi_disjoint]
      <;> aesop
    · <;> simp_all [arrayMeasure, Measure.independent, Measure.prod_null, Measure.infinitePi_disjoint]
      <;> aesop
  
  refine' (indepFun_of_disjoint _ _ h₁)
  <;>
  (try infer_instance) <;>
  (try simp_all [arrayMeasure, Measure.independent, Measure.prod_null, Measure.infinitePi_disjoint]) <;>
  (try aesop)

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₂ : IndepFun (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.1) (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      -- The two components of the product space are independent
      apply indepFun_prod measurable_id measurable_id
    -- The function (fun x ↦ x.1 (n + 1)) is measurable and depends only on the first component
    have h₃ : IndepFun (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      refine' IndepFun.comp_left _ h₂ measurable_id
      intro x y h
      simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₃
  -- The function (fun x ↦ hist alg x n) is measurable and depends only on the second component
  have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ hist alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    refine' IndepFun.comp_right _ h₁ _
    <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  exact h₂

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (ω.1 (n + 1))) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    -- Show that the first component (ω.1) and the second component (ω.2) are independent
    have h₂ : IndepFun Prod.fst Prod.snd (arrayMeasure ν) := indepFun_prod measurable_id measurable_id
    -- Use the fact that the components are independent to deduce the required independence
    have h₃ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (ω.1 (n + 1))) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      apply h₂.comp_right
      <;>
      (try simp_all [indepFun]) <;>
      (try {
        apply Measurable.congr measurable_id
        <;>
        (try simp_all [funext_iff]) <;>
        (try aesop)
      }) <;>
      (try {
        apply Measurable.congr measurable_id
        <;>
        (try simp_all [funext_iff]) <;>
        (try aesop)
      })
    exact h₃
  -- Since (fun ω ↦ ω.2) is independent of (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)), and (fun ω ↦ hist alg ω n) is a function of (fun ω ↦ ω.2),
  -- it follows that (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) and (fun ω ↦ hist alg ω n) are independent.
  have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ hist alg ω n) = (fun ω ↦ ω.2).comp (fun ω ↦ ω) := by
    ext ω
    <;> simp [Function.comp_apply]
    <;> rfl
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁.comp_left (fun ω ↦ ω)
